Retail
Agentic AI Implementation

How a Regional Retail Group Went From Zero AI to 3 Automated Workflows in 60 Days

60
Days to go-live
3
Workflows automated
14 hrs
Saved per week
Month 1
ROI visible
The Challenge

12 Locations. Zero Automation. A Team at Breaking Point.

The client was a fast-growing regional retail group with 12 locations and a lean central operations team. On paper, the business was thriving — sales were up, new sites were opening, and the brand was gaining ground. Behind the scenes, however, the team was cracking under the weight of processes that had never been updated to match their scale.

Inventory tracking was done manually across spreadsheets that were never quite current. When stock ran low at a location, someone had to catch it by eye and make a call. Customer follow-up after purchases was done by hand — a time-consuming process that was falling further and further behind. And every week, a member of the management team spent nearly two full days compiling the weekly sales report from raw data pulled from multiple systems.

Leadership knew AI was the answer. They'd seen competitors move faster and heard the buzz around automation. But nobody on the team had the technical background to evaluate what was possible, what it would cost, or where to start. They needed a partner who could cut through the noise, identify what would actually move the needle, and get it live — fast.

Our Approach

How We Tackled It

STEP 1

AI Opportunity Audit

We spent time inside the business — mapping every manual process, interviewing the people doing the work, and identifying where automation would deliver the highest ROI. From a list of over a dozen candidates, we narrowed it down to three high-impact targets.

STEP 2

Agentic Workflow Design & Build

We designed and built three purpose-built AI agents: an inventory alert agent that monitors stock levels and triggers alerts, a customer re-engagement agent that automatically follows up post-purchase, and a weekly sales report agent that compiles and distributes reporting without human intervention.

STEP 3

Deploy & Train

All three workflows went live within the 60-day commitment. We trained the team on how to work with the agents, handed over full documentation, and stayed close during a 30-day post-launch support period to ensure everything was running smoothly.

The Solution

What We Built

Rather than buying off-the-shelf software that would require expensive customisation, we designed three bespoke agentic AI workflows built around how this business actually operates — its systems, its teams, and its data.

The inventory alert agent connected directly to the client's existing stock management system and began monitoring in real time. The customer re-engagement agent was triggered by purchase events and sent personalised, timely follow-ups without any manual input. The weekly sales report agent pulled data from three separate systems, compiled the report, and distributed it to stakeholders every Monday morning — automatically.

Each agent was tested thoroughly before deployment, and the team was trained to understand what each one does and how to adjust parameters as the business evolves.

Solution Snapshot

  • → Inventory alert AI agent (12-location monitoring)
  • → Customer re-engagement AI agent (post-purchase automation)
  • → Automated weekly sales report (multi-system data pull)
  • → Team training & full workflow documentation
  • → 30-day post-launch support & optimisation
The Results

Numbers That Speak for Themselves

3
AI workflows live in 60 days
14 hrs
Saved per week across the team
Month 1
ROI visible from launch
0
Manual weekly reports produced
"

Lisa and the Neural Horizons team completely changed how we think about AI. We went from zero AI adoption to having three automated workflows running within 60 days. The ROI was visible within the first month.

SM
Sarah M.
CMO, Regional Retail Group
Key Takeaways

What Any Business Can Learn From This

1

You don't need a tech team to start — you need a clear process map.

This client had no in-house AI or technical capability. What they had was a willingness to document how the business actually worked. That was enough to identify three powerful automation opportunities.

2

Start with the most painful manual task — not the most impressive AI use case.

The temptation is to chase the flashiest AI application. The smarter move is to look at what's costing you the most time and energy right now. That's where automation pays back fastest.

3

60 days from zero to live is realistic with the right partner.

AI transformation doesn't have to be a 12-month project. When you focus on clear, high-value use cases and have an implementation partner who knows what they're doing, you can be live — and seeing results — in under two months.
